reviews.review_description:
For the price it's asking I don't think it's currently worth it for the content currently available, I HAVEN'T finished the game yet but as far as I can tell even as a rougelite it's a little lackluster, the characters start with voices then slowly lose their voice overtime only defaulting to one or several voice line(s), unless I'm doing something wrong the end of mission overview if you win or lose looks the same and doesn't display any overview of anything, and there seems to be a texture problem where it's noticable that there are at random times small dashes that show an incorrect color of sorts, I can't currently recommend the game for the amount of content it has but I will update my review if they decide to implement more things to the game.
---
Edit: I've beaten the game and it only took me roughly 4-5 hours because of how unnecessarily difficult the time limited levels were, there is only roughly 3 hours worth of content, once you've beaten the story and all the challenges there is no reason for you to return.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for taking the time to write such a detailed review! We really appreciate the feedback.

A few of the points you raised are things we’re already looking into:

Voice lines: We know they can feel repetitive right now, and we’re planning to expand them in future updates.

End-of-mission overview: You’re right that it doesn’t yet show much detail, we want to make it more informative and rewarding so players can clearly see their progress.

Texture issues: Thanks for pointing out the color dash bug; our team is investigating that so we can get it patched.

Warmly,
The Chrono Hunter Team

reviews.review_description: The gaming experience is fantastic. There are rich map designs, and in the levels I have played, the maps have not been repeated, and I have played many interesting map mechanisms in them. The map guidance is also very simple and intuitive. I have no sense of direction, but I did not get lost. There are many weapons to use, and the wide range of options makes it difficult for me to choose. There are also plenty of upgrade options for bringing my own weapons. I feel like I can keep playing, I love this game so much.

reviews.review_title: A great little rogue lite gem!
reviews.review_description:
Roguelites are pretty numerous in VR ao they have to do something to catch my attention.

Chrono Hunter has some of the the most unique weapons that I've seen in game recently and its still surprising me all the time with its crazy inventive items (think an magicians hat where you can pull out bombs or a farting Unicorn gun !?!?)

It has lots of cool perks you can find on your runs (a lot of which are more interesting than your simple damage/reload/fire rate boost)

Also a unique meta upgrading system where you have make sure you can slot your perks in you upgrade cube.

The game opens up a little way in with levels that really challenge you and are mostly separate from the main story.

A surprisingly robust package and a gem that all rogue lite fans should pick up (fans of action/shooting games should also pick it up Its not the kinda of rogue lite where you start from scratch from death. Very level based)

I'm a huge sucker for good environmental design, and Chrono Hunter absolutely nails it. The lighting, the sound, the way each map feels distinct and lived-in... it's just top-tier stuff. I often find myself just stopping to look around. If you appreciate a game with a strong sense of place, you have to try this.

I have been played this from beta test. I'm not good at FPS game but the maps are not so serious and there are so many different types of weapons to be chosen. Those makes me to play for multiple times. I only hope that they make this to multi-player co-op supported someday.

reviews.review_title: Oh my God this game sucks!!!
reviews.review_description: Super glitchy. Runs super slow. Weapons suck. After playing for a while I tell you the game sucks . And I don’t usually leave bad reviews, but there’s very little I like about this game. It’s made more for toddlers and people who have never played a VR game before. If you’re looking for something fun, anything else will do. Starship troopers blow this one out of the water. Population one runs laps around this. You guys really need to do better. This is just a money grab. Highly disappointed. The producer should be shaking their head, especially bringing them such a great name. They need to go back to the drawing board on this one. But if you like, just shooting and listening to the character, say the same thing again and again. Buy it.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to share your honest feedback. We’re sorry to hear the experience didn’t meet your expectations. Performance, weapon feel, and repetition are all areas we’re actively working on improving, and input like yours helps us prioritize what needs attention. We appreciate you giving the game a try, and we hope you’ll consider checking back in after future updates as we continue refining the experience.
Sincerely.
The CH Team

reviews.review_description: This is one of those games where at first you're like I don't know where this is going and then it gets better and better then you're just like one more round and one more round and one more round for $15 this game is awesome well worth your money good job to the devs can't wait to see where this goes
I love the idea of this game, the npc interaction could be a little more polished, along with movement speed. I love how big the hands are... but theres a lot to do if you take the time to dive in.
This game is fantastic, I recommend it to all roguelite fans! Full FUN !!!! Please add Hungarian subtitles.
A really good game, rich plot, exciting battles, multiple ways to play, super exciting creativity, I really like it
Wanted to experience something new and enjoyed the cartoon like art plant enemy's slime enemy's
Good Graphics, fast Gameplay, cool Upgrades.

It must be more than two years ago I first played a PCVR demo iteration of this game and loved what I saw back then, the devs assured me at the time that it was their intention to bring Chrono Hunter to Standalone VR and here we are... Fabulous work guys, the game itself (although a lot has changed from that which I remember) is still absolute heaps of fun, the locations are glorious, the character models exceptional and the gameplay loop is genuinely great... So why haven't I given it five stars? Well it still needs a bit of polish here and there, although that's not the main reason I held back on the fifth star.  As a playable experience (as I've said) it's great, but as a recorded series to highlight on YouTube (for example) it fails in a few obvious places, firstly (and this might just be me, I'd probably need someone with a bit more technical know-how to guide me here) whenever the characters are speaking or explaining something, if I turn slightly away from them their audio lines are extremely muffled, even though they are clear in-game, they are inaudible on the recording (this could be, as I mentioned above a Quest 3 technical issue, I don't know). Secondly the subtitles are too low on the recorded screen and therefore mostly unreadable (which when coupled with the first issue makes it annoying for a viewer to understand what is happening). Thirdly (and this is definitely something that I'm sure will be improved given time), there is no Mission Stats (well none that I can find) when you arrive back to the lobby, which is a shame as I'd like to know how I did in a particular run, for when I wanted to attempt again (hopefully that makes sense). Fourthly, the other two characters (the blue man with the tail and the rather interestingly attired lady, are not introduced and their functions not explained at all, add to this the fact that 'Buddy' goes and stands next to some weapons after the first tutorial mission... (I ran out of space to write more...)

This is definitely a great game, very refreshing and easy to get started with. The combination of various random weapons always gives me a fresh feeling. In addition to improving the shooting feel, I also hope that the production team can update more maps and enemies in the future. It would be great if they could do this!

Bought this game cause some reviewer mentioned "a strong sense of place".
Have to agree + the first rogue-like mechanics I actually like and makes you want to progress.
Can't recommend this game enough!

I made it through the tutorial stages, and I already like what I see.  I will be posting my gameplay video soon on YouTube @APEboys.
I will update my review once I get further into the game, but as of now, it feels great and is a polished experience.
Very cool game. I won't feel dizzy playing it.
